Your guide to North Norfolk’s most welcoming market town. 

Welcome to Aylsham

Perfectly placed between the City of Norwich and the coast, Aylsham offers a lively town centre, independent shops, cafés and pubs, regular markets and events, scenic walks and cycle routes, and unique attractions such as the Bure Valley Railway and Blickling Hall. Whether you’re planning a relaxed day out or a longer stay, Visit Aylsham has everything you need to plan a memorable visit.

Subscribe and stay informed

Follow us and be the first to know

Just exploring?  Here are our most popular pages ... 

Here’s the most popular pages of the Visit Aylsham website, hopefully making it easy to find what you’re looking for. Whether you’re planning your visit, exploring events, discovering places to eat and shop, or looking for things to see and do, these quick links guide you straight to the information visitors use most. 

What to do

Whether you're a visitor or you're lucky enough to live locally, there's so much to do in and around Aylsham and so much to explore by foot, bike or public transport.

Explore More

Shopping

You’ll find a great selection of independent and unique shops and boutiques with treats for you, treats for your loved ones and treats for your home.

Explore More

Eat and drink

Whatever takes your fancy; the warm welcome of a traditional pub, an evening sipping cocktails, a friendly wine bar or Bistro, you're in for a great lunch or evening out.

Explore More

Where to stay

Make it a weekend or stay longer and explore at your leisure. Here you'll find options to suit all budgets from hotels, B&Bs, self-catering and camping.

Explore More

What's on in Aylsham?

Aylsham hosts a year-round calendar of events that bring the community together and delight visitors. From large-scale celebrations such as the much-loved Christmas lights switch-on to events focused on health and wellbeing, creative workshops and inspiring exhibitions, there is always something happening. Enjoy opportunities to learn a new craft, discover local artistic talent, get social with friends and neighbours, or soak up the atmosphere of live music at the Town Hall and in bars across the town. Whether you’re visiting for a day or staying longer, Aylsham’s events offer a wonderful way to experience the town’s friendly spirit, creativity and sense of community.

Planning further ahead?

Our Events Calendar brings together all upcoming events in one easy-to-use place. Browse what’s happening now or move forward through future months to plan your diary in advance.

View the Events calendar

Aylsham’s Blog is your window into life around the town, sharing the latest news, insights and stories from our vibrant community. Here you’ll find updates on events and projects, features on local people and businesses, reflections on Aylsham’s heritage, and ideas to inspire your visit. Whether you’re planning a trip, staying a while, or simply keen to keep in touch, our blog brings together the voices, creativity and everyday moments that make Aylsham such a welcoming and distinctive place.

View more from our blog...

An Unwelcome Visitor in Aylsham's Gardens

Across Aylsham and our surrounding villages, gardeners have watched in disbelief as carefully clipped hedges, formal borders and treasured topiary have been stripped almost overnight.. But don't despair, as this inspiring blog explains.
Aylsham Town Council
4 min read

Ready, steady, SLOW!

Trainers across the county should now be putting their prized gastropods through their paces. The racetrack has been freshly laundered, conditions are looking good-to-soft, and the coveted Lettuce Pot Trophy is receiving its annual polish. The starting flag will be raised for the Great Aylsham Snail Race at the Food & Craft Market in Aylsham Market Place on Saturday 1 August.
Aylsham Town Council
3 min read

Aylsham Open Gardens 27 & 28 June

Discover the beauty and inspiration of Aylsham’s Open Gardens this June, where 22 unique gardens showcase stunning planting, wildlife-friendly features and creative garden design. From tranquil ponds to tea and cake, enjoy a relaxing weekend exploring hidden green spaces and gathering ideas for your own garden.
Aylsham In Bloom
2 min read

Behind the Studio Doors: Discover the Aylsham Art Trail 2026

There is something magical about stepping inside an artist’s workspace. It is a rare glimpse into the private world of creation, where sketches evolve into finished pieces, kilns cool after firing, paint still dries on the canvas, and ideas take shape in real time. This summer, you don’t just have to imagine it. From Saturday 23rd May to Sunday 7th June 2026, the annual North Norfolk Studios Open Studios event returns. For two inspiring weeks, the county becomes a living gall
Aylsham Town Council
3 min read

Travel the World Without Leaving Norfolk

Through beautiful textiles from around the world, the exhibition explores big ideas such as migration, trade, craftsmanship and global history, revealing how places like Blickling are connected to stories far beyond Norfolk.
Blickling Estate
2 min read

Let's party like it's 1887

This year’s Summer street Party takes place on Sunday, August 23, 2026 and will fill the market place with music, laughter and dancing. However the people of Aylsham have been doing the same thing for more than a century. This fascinating blog reveals how the town has been throwing community parties since Victorian times.
Aylsham Heritage Centre
3 min read

A Tale of Two Towns: Aylsham’s French Connection

Château de Cheverny, nearby the town of La Chaussée-Saint-Victor. In towns and villages across the United Kingdom, signs welcoming visitors often carry the words “twinned with…” followed by the name of a European town. These partnerships are far more than ceremonial titles. They represent decades of friendship, cultural exchange and community connection that continue to bring people together across borders. Like many historic market towns, Aylsham proudly maintains its own Fr
Aylsham Town Council
2 min read

Introducing Aylsham Heritage Centre

Open from March to November, Aylsham Heritage Centre presents a new annual exhibition—this year exploring the rich history of the town’s heart, the Market Place
Aylsham Heritage Centre
2 min read
